Scenario.title: ShadowRay Transportation System Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Intermediate ShadowRay attackers target transportation systems using spear phishing and drive-by compromise techniques to gain initial access, followed by code injection and process hollowing to execute malicious code and maintain persistence.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Device theft", "Weak passwords", "Improper configuration", "Vulnerability", "Internet-facing assets", "Shared DB or directory", "Obsolete products", "Injection flaws", "Custom APIs", "Autonomous system numbers(ASNs)", "SSL Certificates and Attribution", "WHOIS records, contacts and history", "TCP/IP ports", "Public and private cloud", "Web server services such as emails and databases", "Shadow IT", "Poor implementation of access controls", "Malicious insiders", "Social engineering", "Insufficient physical security controls", "Authentication issues"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["External/Removable media", "Loss or Theft of equipment", "Attrition", "Web", "Account takeover", "Inadequate encryption", "Browser-based attack", "Application compromise", "Open port", "Vulnerability exploit", "Ransomware", "Malware", "Man in The Middle(MiTM)", "Session hijacking", "Brute-force attack", "Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS)", "Watering-hole", "Compromised and weak credentials", "SIM Swap", "Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities", "Web forms", "IP address and IP blocks", "Domains and subdomains", "Botnet", "Credential compromise", "Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS)", "Replay Attack", "Cryptanalysis Attacks", "Side Channel Attacks", "Hardware Trojans", "Covert Attacks", "Sleep Deprivation Attack", "Clock Skewing", "Attacks Targeting System Reputation", "Data and Command Integrity Attack", "Eavesdropping", "Command and Control Attacks", "Phishing", "Impersonations/Spoofing", "Improper usage", "Dumpster diving", "Advanced Persistent Threat(APT)", "Baiting", "Shoulder surfing", "Reverse social engineering", "Business Email Compromise(BEC)", "Advanced Fee", "Extortion"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.technique_name: Spear Phishing via Compromised Email Account
stage.description: The attackers send targeted spear phishing emails to transportation system employees with compromised email accounts, tricking them into revealing sensitive information or clicking on malicious links.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit CVE-2020-11652 in the Ray AI framework used by transportation systems for predictive maintenance, compromising the system and gaining initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers execute malicious code to gain control over transportation system infrastructure.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers modify existing processes to evade detection and maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers create scheduled tasks to ensure continuous access and execution.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers utilize compromised credentials or privileges to escalate access within the transportation system.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers use memory-resident malware to avoid detection by traditional security tools.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers modify firmware to persist and evade detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt critical transportation services to cause maximum impact.

Scenario.summary: ShadowRay attackers employ sophisticated tactics to compromise transportation systems, including spear phishing, drive-by compromise, code injection, and process hollowing. They maintain persistence through scheduled tasks and abuse of elevated privileges, evading detection with fileless malware and system firmware modification. Finally, they cause significant impact through denial-of-service attacks.
